title: Canon Adjudication Audit Summary
description: Structured stdout of canon_adjudication_audit.py -- exactly ONE JSON line, either shape depending on how the script was invoked. When --check is given (alone or combined with --init, mode 'check'/'init+check'), the CHECK branch below applies: the four required-item categories (duplicate_source_form/existing_merge/candidate_missed_merge_pair/review_queue_unresolved) recomputed fresh from canon.json, cross-checked against canon_adjudications.json. When --init is given WITHOUT --check (mode 'init'), the INIT branch applies instead -- a much smaller shape reporting only whether the empty template was written and what was already on file. The two shapes are mutually exclusive per run (never both printed) and discriminated here by 'mode'.

canon_present.description: False iff canon.json does not exist -- canon *presence* is canon_validate.py's job, never a silent green; totals are all-zero and gate_passed is true in that case.

advisory.description: True iff --advisory was given -- forces exit 0 even when gate_passed is false. Never affects a genuine fatal (fatal never reaches stdout at all).

totals.description: required_items and by_kind cover all 4 categories. confirmed_ok/missing_verdict/adverse/invalid_verdict_class cover categories 1-3 only (review_queue_unresolved never uses the confirmed_ok/adverse mechanism). cap_notes/cap_overrides_ok/cap_overrides_missing are 0 or 1 (canon is project-global -- exactly one cap scope, '__canon__'). review_queue_items/review_queue_unaccepted are category 4's own counters.

orphaned_records.description: Adjudication/cap-override/queue-risk-override records that no longer correspond to any currently-required item. Informational, non-blocking, safe to prune -- never counted in blocking_count.

blocking_count.description: Rollup invariant (procedurally enforced by the script, not schema-expressible): blocking_count == totals.missing_verdict + totals.adverse + totals.invalid_verdict_class + totals.cap_overrides_missing + totals.review_queue_unaccepted.

warnings.description: Human-readable, non-blocking notes -- data-quality warnings (e.g. an entries{} map key mismatched against its own source_form field), orphaned-record notices, and (when category 3's pair count exceeds pair_review_cap) a copy-pasteable CAP-NOTE carrying the fresh cap-identity a degenerate_cap_overrides['__canon__'] record must match.

created.description: True iff a fresh empty template was actually written this run (the file was absent, or --force reset an existing one).
